sample_comments: While the auction itself is straight-forward, the information provided to contact the seller once an item is won is lacking major overhaul.  I had to call the corporate office of the sellers company in another state other than the items location.  Have their CSR give me the phone number for the location, then call numerous times over 3 day period with no answer.  While this may seem minuscule, the low cost flight opportunities for me to get to the items location disappeared and I had to spend 200.oo more to get to that location in less than 48 hours.  I clearly see its the sellers providing the information, HOWEVER, a strict guidelines for what the sellers MUST POST to enable bid winners to contact the seller in a timely manner to arrangemnt pick-up of the item.

I have been pleased with most purchases until the last one that we purchased a 2007 Peterbilt from Collinsville OK.  The auction yard left it out of the because I could not get there  by closing time, which was nice.  However, the truck batteries were completely dead, so dead that 2 sets of jumper cables would not start it, it still had enough air pressure that I was able to have my wife pull me with my pickup to get it started.  Then the clutch was so still that I had to put my foot under the pedal to pull it up to engage it.  I don' mind those things but am very troubled by the fact that it was not disclosed and am disappointed in Purple Wave for that.  Always in past experiences, Purple wave has be very accurate with inspections and reporting flaws.  I know that the batteries did not just die that dead in one day and the clutch did not get that stiff in one day.  I was able to get it home but my wife had to pull me to start it 3 times.

The buying and paying process is great. When I recommend you to people I warn them to be wary of descriptions. I'm a busy business owner so going hundreds of miles to look at different pieces of equipment each week would be counterproductive. I use descriptions and contact with the seller to make an informed decision about the item. Most of the time, the seller and description do not give whole truths. I could care less if the item has a cracked windshield noted but the bad transmssion or fuel pump isn't noted. I know the listing agents aren't mechanics...but they should be if they are giving any type of mechanical descriptions. I've bought trucks that had completely different engines than the listings showed. I've bought a grader that was listed with engine trouble. It was only mentioned when I arrived to pick up the machine that it had been ran out of oil and the crankshaft was wiped out. 

All this to say...I've learned how to buy and usually what is safe to buy. I love purplewave and know at what pric
You can keep your mine busy
exelente Citio wed, facil de usar
